steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts the old view of “sparse, widely distributed population” with the new conclusion of “urbanism in the Amazon basin.” The shift from sparse to urbanism is the key signal.

steps.title: Match the finding to urbanism
steps.content:
Urbanism requires organized, concentrated settlement with significant infrastructure.

Choice D shows “monumental platforms and dense central settlements linked to smaller settlements by a system of canals and roadways”—this directly demonstrates the organized, concentrated development that urbanism entails.

Choices A, B, and C either describe sparse settlements, external connections, or scattered farms without coordinated infrastructure.

This proves that Casarabe people invested in monumental, organized development rather than remaining sparse and dispersed.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
